title: Iran Declares Hormuz Open — Then Reverses — Oil Swings 12% in Hours
detail: April 17 saw dramatic whipsawing in the Iran crisis. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the Strait of Hormuz 'open to all commercial shipping traffic' for the duration of an Israeli-Lebanon ceasefire brokered that day. Oil prices crashed roughly 11% immediately — WTI fell to ~$79.78, Brent to ~$86.84 — and Trump confirmed on Truth Social that the Strait was open. But within hours, Iran's Parliamentary Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf reversed the statement, declaring the Strait was NOT open as long as the US maintained its blockade of Iranian ports and had allowed Israel to continue Lebanon operations under the ceasefire agreement. PBS reported Iranian military forces reimposed restrictions. The Macron-Starmer multilateral shipping protection summit was held online the same day. A 10-day US-brokered Israel-Hezbollah truce entered effect April 17, with ceasefire expiry set for April 21.

media.caption: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open, then reverses — oil whipsaws 12% on April 17, 2026

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Fast-Tracking Psychedelic Drug Research for Veterans
detail: President Trump signed an executive order on April 17, 2026 directing the FDA to fast-track reviews and expand access to psychedelic drugs — including psilocybin, ibogaine, LSD, and MDMA — specifically citing mental health treatment for veterans with PTSD and depression. The signing ceremony in the Oval Office included HHS Secretary RFK Jr., podcast host Joe Rogan, and senior federal health officials. The FDA simultaneously announced the first ibogaine investigational new drug clearance, paving the way for US human clinical trials. The DEA and DOJ were directed to initiate rescheduling reviews for any Schedule I substance completing successful Phase 3 clinical trials. The move represented a significant shift in US drug policy, with bipartisan support from veterans advocacy groups.

media.caption: Trump signs executive order directing FDA fast-track for psychedelic drug research to treat veteran PTSD — April 17, 2026
